did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper titled 'Multimodal skin lesion classification in dermoscopy and clinical images using a hierarchical attention fusion network' (Chunk 3-4) explicitly uses a novel hierarchical attention fusion network, a deep learning method based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), to classify skin lesions (basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, nevus, squamous cell carcinoma, and seborrheic keratosis) using multimodal dermoscopy and clinical images.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The provided text contains multiple abstracts from the Journal of Investigative Dermatology (2021) that report original experimental and observational studies. Specifically, Chunk 3-4 describes a study proposing a novel hierarchical attention fusion network for multimodal skin lesion classification, including dataset construction (1907 image pairs) and performance metrics (81.2% accuracy). Chunk 0-2 details a retrospective cross-sectional review of the National Health Interview Survey regarding health information technology utilization. Chunk 3-4 also presents an observational cohort study of 1525 systemic sclerosis patients analyzing occupational exposures. These are original research studies, not reviews.

title: Association of occupational exposures with disease manifestations in systemic sclerosis
brief_summary: This study investigates the association between occupational exposures and disease manifestations in systemic sclerosis (SSc) using data from the Canadian Scleroderma Research Group cohort. The researchers analyzed 1525 patients to compare clinical features, antibody profiles, and mortality rates across different occupational exposure histories, including silica, organic solvents, and industrial fumes. Logistic regression models were employed to determine the clinical characteristics associated with each specific occupational exposure. The results indicated that occupational SSc is more prevalent in males and is linked to more severe disease phenotypes, such as diffuse SSc, renal disease, and interstitial lung disease. Specific exposures like silica were associated with increased mortality, while epoxy resin exposure correlated with anti-RNA polymerase III antibody positivity. The study concludes that obtaining detailed occupational histories is imperative for secondary prevention and risk education in SSc patients.

all_task_types.target: Multimodal skin lesion classes (basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, nevus, squamous cell carcinoma, and seborrheic keratosis)
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes a hierarchical attention fusion network (CNN) for multimodal skin lesion classification using dermoscopy and clinical images. The model predicts discrete diagnostic categories for five common skin diseases, achieving an average accuracy of 81.2% on a dataset of 1907 image pairs.

datasets_used.name: Canadian Scleroderma Research Group cohort
datasets_used.description: A cohort of systemic sclerosis (SSc) patients in Canada, with data extracted over the years 2003-2019. It includes data on gender, occupational exposure history, symptom severity, antibody profile, and mortality.
classes_used: Occupational exposure types: organic solvents, industrial fumes, silica, heavy metals, asbestos, epoxy resins
Disease manifestations: diffuse SSc, renal disease, interstitial lung disease (ILD), mortality, anti-RNA polymerase III antibody positivity, anti-centromere antibody status

use.other: Retrospective cross-sectional analysis to determine clinical characteristics associated with occupational exposures using logistic regression models.

datasets_used.name: Multimodal skin lesion dataset
datasets_used.description: A constructed dataset of multimodal image pairs (dermoscopy and clinical images) collected from Xiangya Hospital of Central South University over the recent 10 years. It covers five common skin diseases.

use.training: Used to validate the effectiveness of the proposed hierarchical attention fusion network (CNN) for multimodal skin lesion classification.
use.other: The dataset was used to evaluate the model's performance, achieving an average accuracy of 81.2%.

datasets_used.name: National Health Interview Survey (NHIS)
datasets_used.description: A retrospective cross-sectional review dataset from 2011-2018 used to analyze health information technology (HIT) utilization among skin cancer patients.
classes_used: HIT utilization behaviors (scheduling appointments online, looking up health information, communicating by email, filling prescriptions electronically)

use.other: Used for retrospective cross-sectional analysis to assess associations between sociodemographic characteristics and HIT utilization.

datasets_used.name: Minneapolis VA Medical Center Dermatology Clinic Cohort
datasets_used.description: A pre/post survey study cohort of 203 patients in the dermatology clinic from August 2019 to October 2020, used to evaluate attitudes towards active surveillance for basal cell carcinoma (BCC) before and after viewing an educational video.
classes_used: Concerns regarding BCC (tumor growth, metastases, frequent doctor visits, making wrong decision)

use.other: Used for a pre/post survey study to determine changes in patient concerns and comfort levels regarding BCC active surveillance.

datasets_used.name: Santa Maria alle Scotte Hospital / Italian National Pharmacovigilance Network Cohort
datasets_used.description: A study population of 263 patients with melanoma treated with biological drugs (Nivolumab and Pembrolizumab) from January 2019 to October 2020, analyzing adverse drug reactions (ADRs).

use.other: Used for a statistical study of adverse drug reactions, comparing ADRs detected by medical record analysis and reported to the Italian National Pharmacovigilance Network.

datasets_used.name: Castle Biosciences Inc. Stage I Cutaneous Melanoma Cohort
datasets_used.description: A cohort of 850 patients with stage I cutaneous melanoma (CM) used for the 31-GEP test analysis. A subset of 325 patients received a negative sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNBx) result.
classes_used: 31-GEP Risk Classes: Class 1A (low risk), Class 1B/2A (intermediate risk), Class 2B (high risk)

use.other: Used to perform the 31-GEP test and assess 5-year recurrence-free survival (RFS) using Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazards analyses.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To analyze associations between sociodemographic characteristics and health information technology (HIT) utilization among skin cancer patients.
To determine clinical characteristics associated with each occupational exposure in systemic sclerosis patients.
To quantify the association between specific occupational exposures (silica, organic solvents, asbestos) and disease manifestations (diffuse SSc, renal disease, mortality, ILD) in systemic sclerosis.
To compare the proportion of patients with specific concerns regarding basal cell carcinoma active surveillance pre- and post-video (e.g., tumor growth, metastases, frequent doctor visits).
To assess 5-year recurrence-free survival (RFS) in patients with stage I cutaneous melanoma stratified by 31-GEP risk classes.
To assess 5-year recurrence-free survival and identify independent predictors of recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.
To determine if Class 2B GEP result was an independent predictor of recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.

statistical_methods_used.method: Sensitivity and Negative Predictive Value (NPV) calculation
statistical_methods_used.purpose: To evaluate the diagnostic performance of the 31-GEP test and sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNBx) for predicting recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.

hypothesis_testing.test: Logistic regression, Chi-square test (implied by p-values for proportions), Cox proportional hazards analysis, Kaplan-Meier analysis
hypothesis_testing.null_hypothesis: There is no association between occupational exposures and disease manifestations in systemic sclerosis; there is no difference in health information technology utilization across sociodemographic groups; there is no difference in patient concerns regarding basal cell carcinoma active surveillance pre- and post-video; Class 2B GEP result is not an independent predictor of recurrence.
hypothesis_testing.alternative_hypothesis: Occupational exposures are associated with specific disease manifestations and mortality in systemic sclerosis; sociodemographic characteristics are associated with health information technology utilization; an educational video changes patient concerns regarding basal cell carcinoma active surveillance; Class 2B GEP result is an independent predictor of recurrence.
hypothesis_testing.interpretation: Statistical significance was determined using p-values. For occupational exposures, logistic regression models yielded odds ratios with confidence intervals not crossing 1.0 (e.g., silica exposure OR 1.19, CI 1.08-1.32; organic solvents OR 1.05, CI 1.01-1.08; asbestos OR 1.16, CI 1.06-1.26), indicating significant associations. For health information technology utilization, p<0.05 was used to identify significant differences across sociodemographic lines. For patient attitudes, p-values <0.05 (e.g., p=0.0065, p<0.0001, p=0.0070) indicated significant changes in concerns post-video. For melanoma risk stratification, p<0.001 indicated significant differences in recurrence-free survival and independent prediction by GEP class.

National Health Interview Survey (NHIS).notes: Proportion of patients scheduling appointments online increased from 4.36% to 21.35%. Proportion looking up health info online increased from 48.89% to 58.21%. Proportion communicating by email increased from 6.32% to 26.01%. Proportion filling prescriptions electronically increased from 12.05% to 17.86%.

Castle Biosciences Inc. Stage I Cutaneou.notes: Multivariable analysis showed Class 2B GEP result was an independent predictor of recurrence. Class 1A had an NPV of 98% for the whole cohort, compared with an NPV of 95% for the SLNBx-assessed subset (N=222).

architecture_details.description: A novel multimodal CNN that utilizes an attention fusion block to learn refined single-modality features through attention information in the complementary imaging modality. It integrates extracted single-modality features hierarchically in each stage of the network.

training_details.dataset_description: Multimodal image pairs (dermoscopy and clinical images) collected from Xiangya Hospital of Central South University over the recent 10 years

classification_or_output_head.details: Predicts one of five common skin disease categories

conclusions: Occupational systemic sclerosis is more prevalent in males (1:1.5 male-to-female ratio) compared to non-occupational systemic sclerosis (1:6 ratio).
Silica exposure is associated with a higher prevalence of diffuse systemic sclerosis (OR 1.19, CI 1.08-1.32) and increased mortality (OR 1.15, CI 1.06-1.25).
Exposure to organic solvents is associated with renal disease (OR 1.05, CI 1.01-1.08).
Asbestos exposure is associated with increased mortality (OR 1.16, CI 1.06-1.26).
Industrial fumes and heavy metal exposure are associated with higher prevalence of interstitial lung disease, renal disease, and mortality.
Epoxy resin exposure is associated with interstitial lung disease, renal disease, diffuse systemic sclerosis, and anti-RNA polymerase III antibody positivity.
Lower frequency of anti-centromere antibody was observed in patients exposed to silica, heavy metals, or industrial fumes.

Patients with previous occupational exposure to organic solvents, industrial fumes, silica, heavy metals, asbestos, and epoxy resins predominantly exhibit more severe disease phenotypes and/or mortality.
It remains imperative to obtain a detailed occupational history in systemic sclerosis patients to focus on secondary prevention and risk education.
A novel hierarchical attention fusion network for multimodal skin lesion classification achieved an average accuracy of 81.2% on a dataset of 1907 image pairs.
The proposed hierarchical attention fusion network outperformed a simple late-fusion based multimodal method, which achieved an average accuracy of 77.3%.

The hierarchical attention fusion network utilizes an attention fusion block to learn refined single-modality features through attention information in the complementary imaging modality and integrates features hierarchically.
Providing education on basal cell carcinoma may alleviate patients’ concerns regarding active surveillance.
The 31-GEP test stratifies 5-year metastatic risk in patients with stage I cutaneous melanoma with a higher negative predictive value than sentinel lymph node biopsy.

Class 1A 31-GEP result had a 98% 5-year recurrence-free survival and an NPV of 98%, while Class 2B result had a 76% 5-year recurrence-free survival and was an independent predictor of recurrence (HR 5.8; p<0.001).
important_remarks: The provided PDF is a collection of abstracts from the Journal of Investigative Dermatology (2021), not a single cohesive study, containing distinct research on systemic sclerosis, skin lesion classification, health information technology, basal cell carcinoma attitudes, melanoma adverse drug reactions, and melanoma risk stratification.

The systemic sclerosis study (Chunk 3-4) is a retrospective cross-sectional analysis of the Canadian Scleroderma Research Group cohort (N=1525, 2003-2019), which limits causal inference regarding occupational exposures.

The multimodal skin lesion classification study (Chunk 3-4) uses a self-constructed dataset of 1907 image pairs from a single institution (Xiangya Hospital), raising concerns about external validity and generalizability.
The melanoma risk stratification study (Chunk 5-6) relies on the 31-GEP test from Castle Biosciences Inc., indicating potential commercial sponsorship or conflict of interest.
The melanoma adverse drug reaction study (Chunk 5-6) is limited to a single Italian hospital cohort (Santa Maria alle Scotte) over a short period (Jan 2019-Oct 2020), limiting generalizability.
The basal cell carcinoma active surveillance study (Chunk 1-2) is a pre/post survey of 203 patients at a single VA Medical Center, lacking a control group and long-term follow-up.
The health information technology study (Chunk 1-2) uses retrospective NHIS data (2011-2018), which may suffer from self-reporting bias and does not capture actual usage behavior directly.
